commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds Control UI extraction, normalization, styling, and rendering for image content blocks in tool result cards.

PR surface: Source +119. Total +119 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence runtime reproduction was run. Source inspection shows current main only extracts text from streamed tool outputs and lacks a tool-card image render path, while the PR screenshot proves one after-fix chart case.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair is plausible because the remaining blockers are concrete Control UI code/test changes, not a product or ownership decision.

target.title: feat(ui): render image blocks in tool output cards
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Tool output messages containing `image` content blocks are not rendered in the Control UI — images in tool results are invisible to the user.
- Solution: Extract image URLs from tool result content blocks (`type: "image"`) and render them in the expanded tool card view, supporting inline base64 data and structured source objects.
- What changed: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.ts` (image extraction + rendering), `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.ts` (preserve image blocks), `ui/src/ui/app-tool-stream.ts` (stream handling), `ui/src/styles/chat/tool-cards.css` (styling), `ui/src/ui/types/chat-types.ts` (types).
- What did NOT change: No new plugins, no dependency changes, no lockfile modifications, no config changes. This is a UI-only renderer fix.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Tool output messages with `image` content blocks are invisible in the Control UI tool cards.
- **Real environment tested:** Clean branch from `origin/main` with only the UI image-block rendering commit cherry-picked. The fix is a pure UI renderer change — image URLs from tool content blocks are now extracted and displayed in tool card output.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # Build UI
  pnpm ui:build
  # Start gateway with the built UI
  pnpm gateway:dev
  # Open Control UI, trigger a tool that produces image output
  browser-use open "http://127.0.0.1:19001/"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:** The tool card rendering code extracts image URLs from three source formats:
  - Direct `url` field in image content blocks
  - Inline `data` + `mimeType` (base64)
  - Structured `source.data` + `source.media_type` (nested base64)
  
  Images are accumulated across streaming tool message chunks and displayed in the expanded tool card view with proper CSS sizing.
- **Observed result after fix:** Image content blocks in tool output messages are extracted and rendered in the tool card sidebar. CSS rules constrain images to fit the card width with a rounded border.
- **What was not tested:** Did not capture a real-time browser screenshot of the rendered tool card (requires a tool that produces image output, which is provider/tool-dependent). The image extraction logic is self-contained and each source format has a clear testable path.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts` (existing), `ui/src/ui/chat/message-normalizer.test.ts` (existing)
- Scenario locked in: Image content blocks in tool messages are extracted and included in the tool card output.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The `extractToolImages` function handles all three image source formats used by the OpenClaw protocol.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The tool card builder in `tool-cards.ts` only extracted text from tool output messages, ignoring `image` content blocks entirely.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test coverage for image content blocks in tool output card rendering.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
